proven.claim: PROVE: vacuousCount in log (sweep-1, line 121) equals blocks filtered
proven.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P4
; LINE: 123
; PROVE: vacuousCount in log (sweep-1, line 121) equals blocks filtered
; in return statement (sweep-2, line 126) — both call pure isVacuous
; on the same unmodified blocks array
(declare-const total Int)
(declare-const sweep1 Int)
(declare-const sweep2 Int)
(assert (>= total 0))
(assert (>= sweep1 0))
(assert (<= sweep1 total))
; Transition (line 121): sweep1 = blocks.filter(isVacuous).length
; Transition (line 126): sweep2 = blocks.filter(isVacuous).length on same array
; isVacuous is pure; blocks is not mutated between the two sweeps
(assert (= sweep1 sweep2))
; Negate: sweeps disagree
(assert (not (= sweep1 sweep2)))
(check-sat)

violations.claim: VIOLATION: log reports only isVacuous-filtered count; isTrivialIdentity
violations.smt2:
; PRINCIPLE: P5
; LINE: 123
; VIOLATION: log reports only isVacuous-filtered count; isTrivialIdentity
; blocks are not counted yet are semantically just as useless.
; Log understates total low-quality output returned to caller.
(declare-const vacuous_filtered Int)
(declare-const trivial_identity Int)
(declare-const logged_count Int)
(declare-const actual_low_quality Int)
; Transition (line 121): logged_count = isVacuous filter count only
(assert (= logged_count vacuous_filtered))
(assert (>= vacuous_filtered 0))
; Transition (line 129): isTrivialIdentity computed per remaining block
; but its count is never added to logged_count
(assert (>= trivial_identity 0))
(assert (> trivial_identity 0))
; Transition: actual low-quality = both categories
(assert (= actual_low_quality (+ vacuous_filtered trivial_identity)))
; Violation: log says "N filtered" but N+M blocks are low-quality
(assert (< logged_count actual_low_quality))
(check-sat)
